title: henriettea tovarensis
intro: Henriettea tovarensis, also known as Tovare rose, is a flowering plant species in the family Melastomataceae, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Henriettea tovarensis is prepared in various herbal forms, including tea infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments.
